title: Deploy with Stripe Projects
description: Configure and deploy your local project using the Stripe Projects CLI plugin.

steps.title: Detect project setup
steps.description: Ensure the projects plugin is available and scan the codebase.

nodes.title: Check for Stripe Projects plugin
nodes.description: Run `stripe projects --help`. If it returns 'unknown command', install it with `stripe plugin install projects`. Confirm it's available before proceeding.

nodes.title: Scan project and plan deployment
nodes.description: Explore the codebase: identify framework, existing hosting config, database usage, auth providers, env vars. Summarize findings — don't ask the developer to explain what you can read from the code.

steps.title: Initialize project
steps.description: Link your code to Stripe Projects.

nodes.title: Initialize Stripe Project
nodes.description: Run `stripe projects init --help` to see available flags, then run `stripe projects init` with appropriate options for the detected framework. Report what was detected and what stripe.json contains.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the command completed successfully and the reported output matches the step description.

steps.title: Configure secrets
steps.description: Set production API keys and environment variables.

nodes.title: Set Stripe keys as project secrets
nodes.description: Run `stripe whoami --format json` only to confirm the CLI is authenticated and whether test key access is available; it does not print key material. Run `stripe projects secrets --help` to discover the command interface. If STRIPE_SECRET_KEY or STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY are needed, ask the developer to provide or securely set those values, then report which secrets were configured without printing secret values.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the command completed successfully and the reported output matches the step description.

nodes.title: Set additional secrets
nodes.description: Scan .env, .env.local, .env.example for variables NOT already handled (not STRIPE_ keys). If NONE remain → SKIP. If found → use `stripe projects secrets set` to configure them. Ask the developer for production values only if they differ from what's in .env.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the command completed successfully and the reported output matches the step description.

nodes.title: Run first deployment
nodes.description: Run `stripe projects deploy --help` to check available flags, then run `stripe projects deploy`. Report the build output and deployment URL.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the command completed successfully and the reported output matches the step description.

nodes.title: Verify deployment is live
nodes.description: Ask the developer to visit the deployment URL and confirm it works. If it's a payment flow, suggest testing with card number 4242424242424242.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the described test flow and confirm the expected result is observable.

steps.title: Configure production webhooks
steps.description: Register webhook endpoint for the deployed app.

nodes.title: Register webhook endpoint
nodes.description: Look for webhook handling code (stripe.webhooks.constructEvent, /webhook routes). If found → construct the production URL (deployment URL + webhook path) and use `stripe projects webhooks --help` to discover how to register it. Set STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET as a project secret and report the registered endpoint. If NOT found → SKIP.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the command completed successfully and the reported output matches the step description.
